labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose
labs.actionRequired: Reassess concomitant medications including NSAIDs; consider discontinuation
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for nephrotoxic effects and acute kidney injury when used concomitantly with ACEi or ARB

labs.frequency: within 2-4 weeks after starting or changing dose
labs.actionRequired: Review concurrent drugs; counsel to discontinue NSAIDs if present
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for hyperkalemia when used concomitantly with ACEi or ARB as NSAIDs can impair kidney excretion of potassium

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Particularly important in patients with CKD, diabetes, decompensated heart failure, volume depletion, or advanced age who are at increased risk for hyperkalemia and nephrotoxicity
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: History of the use of over-the-counter nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), supplements, and herbal treatments should be pursued, and patients should be counseled to discontinue these remedies if present.
